paragraphs.text:
Our speaker roster is curated by working engineers, not committees. We prioritize
      practitioner experience over academic credentials: every talk this year comes
      from someone who has shipped, broken, and rebuilt the systems they're describing.
      Expect concrete war stories, postmortems, and architectures you can take home on Monday.
Walks through a four-region failover redesign that cut MTTR by 78%. Includes the parts that didn't work.
A field guide to debugging kernel-level packet drops on commodity NICs. Real captures, real numbers, no marketing.
How a 200-engineer org migrated off a monorepo without freezing the trunk. Lessons in incremental tooling.
Capacity modeling when your traffic graph has fractal seasonality. Practical math for honest forecasts.
Designing a custom storage chassis for sustained 40GB/s writes. Thermals, vibration, and the BOM behind it.
A six-quarter retrospective on multi-tenant noisy-neighbor isolation. What worked, what we ripped out.
Building observability that survives the outage it's meant to diagnose. Patterns for out-of-band telemetry.
Pushing real-time inference onto $4 microcontrollers. Quantization tradeoffs and a brutally honest power budget.
Replacing a 12-year-old sharding layer without a maintenance window. The dual-write playbook, with receipts.
Error budgets as a negotiation tool, not a scoreboard. How to keep product teams honest without becoming the police.
BGP route leaks in practice: three incidents, three root causes, and the guardrails we now run on every edge.
Threat modeling for control planes that touch physical infrastructure. A pragmatic checklist that survived an audit.

After a decade running multi-region infrastructure, Dana argues that "resilience" is a
            misleading word — what we actually want is graceful degradation under conditions
            nobody designed for. This keynote walks through three real incidents and the
            architectural patterns that turned each one into a non-event by the second year.
Keynote   Hall A — Main Stage  ·  50 min + Q&A

Eventual consistency is the default escape hatch for distributed systems — but the
            bill arrives slowly, in support tickets, reconciliation jobs, and 3 AM pages. Jian
            unpacks the operational debt that compounds quietly behind "AP" systems, and
            offers a decision framework for when stronger guarantees are worth the latency.
Keynote   Hall A — Main Stage  ·  50 min + Q&A
Two days of deep systems engineering — Pittsburgh, PA.
October 9–10, 2025 · David L. Lawrence Convention Center

DH
        Dana Halverson
        Principal SRE · GridForge
        Walks through a four-region failover redesign that cut MTTR by 78%. Includes the parts that didn't work.

MO
        Marcus Okafor
        Staff Engineer · PulseLabs
        A field guide to debugging kernel-level packet drops on commodity NICs. Real captures, real numbers, no marketing.

        Sofia Reinhardt
        Platform Lead · Alloy Systems
        How a 200-engineer org migrated off a monorepo without freezing the trunk. Lessons in incremental tooling.

        Jian Tao
        Distinguished Engineer · CoreStack
        Capacity modeling when your traffic graph has fractal seasonality. Practical math for honest forecasts.

        Hardware Architect · Ironvault
        Designing a custom storage chassis for sustained 40GB/s writes. Thermals, vibration, and the BOM behind it.

        Infra Director · Tangent IO
        A six-quarter retrospective on multi-tenant noisy-neighbor isolation. What worked, what we ripped out.

        Kira Whitmore
        Senior Engineer · GridForge
        Building observability that survives the outage it's meant to diagnose. Patterns for out-of-band telemetry.

NA
        Noah Ardelean
        Embedded Lead · PulseLabs
        Pushing real-time inference onto $4 microcontrollers. Quantization tradeoffs and a brutally honest power budget.

YM
        Yuki Matsuda
        Database Engineer · CoreStack
        Replacing a 12-year-old sharding layer without a maintenance window. The dual-write playbook, with receipts.

        Priya Ghosh
        Reliability Lead · Alloy Systems
        Error budgets as a negotiation tool, not a scoreboard. How to keep product teams honest without becoming the police.

TC

        Network Engineer · Ironvault
        BGP route leaks in practice: three incidents, three root causes, and the guardrails we now run on every edge.

AL
        Amelia Larsson
        Security Engineer · Tangent IO
        Threat modeling for control planes that touch physical infrastructure. A pragmatic checklist that survived an audit.

DH
        
          Day 1 Opening Keynote · Wed Oct 9, 9:00 AM
          Dana Halverson
          Principal Site Reliability Engineer · GridForge
          Designing for Failure at Scale

JT
        
          Day 2 Opening Keynote · Thu Oct 10, 9:00 AM
          Jian Tao
          Distinguished Engineer · CoreStack
          The Hidden Cost of Eventual Consistency
